description: Manual or automated output-quality eval tasks for TensorPM office artifact skills. Run with and without the relevant skill, then score the produced artifact against the rubric.

tasks.prompt: Create a 7-slide PowerPoint steering committee status deck from a project with milestones, budget variance, top risks, decisions needed, and next steps.

rubric.criterion: Deck structure matches the steering use case and stays within the requested slide count.
Every slide title states a concrete takeaway rather than a generic topic.
Layout is readable with bounded text, consistent margins, no overloaded slide, and reusable design tokens/constants.
Project facts, dates, owners, currencies, and assumptions are explicit and not invented.
File is written via outputPath with a descriptive project-derived name.

tasks.prompt: Create a client-facing PowerPoint decision deck comparing three delivery options, their tradeoffs, risks, recommendation, and action request.

rubric.criterion: Decision framing is clear and leads to a specific recommendation.
Options and tradeoffs are visualized in readable comparison slides.
Risk, owner, mitigation, and decision deadline are present where relevant.
Slide density, typography, and content-specific visual direction avoid generic AI-deck defaults.
Appendix or backup material is separated from the main story.

tasks.prompt: Create an editable Excel workbook for project budget tracking with input assumptions, planned vs actual costs, forecast, variance, and summary.

rubric.criterion: Workbook separates summary, inputs, calculations/details, and assumptions.
Editable cells, assumptions, and formulas are intentional, visible, and documented.
Totals reconcile between detail rows and summary.
Dates, currencies, units, and status values use consistent formatting.
Limitations such as missing data or unsupported charts are visible.

tasks.prompt: Export project action items to a management XLSX workbook with summary metrics, detail rows, owners, due dates, status, priority, and assumptions.

rubric.criterion: Rows match the selected project action-item source data.
Summary counts reconcile with detail rows.
Columns are readable, labeled with units where needed, and not unnecessarily wide.
Assumptions and filters are included on a visible sheet.
Workbook is useful for later editing rather than only a raw dump.

tasks.prompt: Create a Word meeting summary from project notes with decisions, action items, owners, due dates, open questions, and a concise discussion summary.

rubric.criterion: Document structure separates context, decisions, action items, discussion, and open questions.
Action items include owner, due date, status, and source confidence where available.
Narrative is concise and uses the user's language.
Semantic headings and tables make the document editable.
Missing inputs are marked rather than inferred.

tasks.prompt: Write a formal editable DOCX decision memo covering background, decision question, options, tradeoffs, recommendation, impact, risks, and next action.

rubric.criterion: Decision question and recommendation are clear in the first page.
Options, tradeoffs, impact, and risks are structured for comparison.
Document uses semantic headings and simple editable tables.
Project facts and assumptions are explicit and source-consistent.
Next action has owner and deadline when data exists.

tasks.prompt: Create a printable client PDF status pack with title band, executive summary, milestones, risks, decisions, next steps, and source notes. Preview as PNG before final PDF.

rubric.criterion: HTML-to-PNG preview loop is used before final PDF export when available.
Final PDF has readable typography, consistent margins, and no overlaps/clipping.
Page breaks do not split headings or key tables awkwardly.
Status colors are paired with text labels and print legibly.
File name and source notes are descriptive and project-derived.

tasks.prompt: Create a fixed-layout PDF risk report for print with high-priority risks, impact, owner, mitigation, escalation need, and assumptions.

rubric.criterion: Report includes all requested risk fields and separates assumptions from facts.
Visual hierarchy makes high-priority risks easy to scan.
PDF layout is previewed and free of overlap, clipping, unreadable tables, and token drift.
Headers, footers, dates, and units are consistent.
Large raw tables are summarized or moved out of the PDF.

tasks.prompt: Review the generated PDF status pack against the original client request. Return blocker/major/minor findings first and do not regenerate unless asked.

rubric.criterion: Review uses the original request and available artifact evidence, not generic taste.
Findings are severity-ranked and actionable with concrete structured evidence and fix instructions.
PDF-specific layout issues such as clipping, page breaks, and print readability are checked.
Reviewer distinguishes must-fix blockers from optional polish.
Limitations of the review evidence are explicit.

tasks.prompt: Review the generated Excel workbook for formula correctness, summary/detail reconciliation, formatting consistency, and visible assumptions.

rubric.criterion: Review checks reconciliation between summary metrics and detail rows.
Formula, hardcoded-assumption, and editable-cell risks are identified precisely.
Assumptions, missing inputs, dates, currencies, and units are evaluated.
Findings are severity-ranked with specific remediation steps.
Reviewer avoids dumping raw workbook content into chat.
